fields.summary: Confluence space permissions not working - users can see pages they should not
fields.description: Users in the engineering group can see pages in our finance-internal Confluence space even though I have set the space permissions to restrict access to only the finance-team group. I set the space permissions last week via Space Settings > Permissions but it does not seem to be taking effect. This is a compliance concern as the finance space contains salary information.

content.text: I checked the space permissions and finance-team group has View permission. But the engineering group also has View permission granted at the global site level. I think the global permissions are overriding the space-level restrictions.

Confirmed. In Confluence Cloud, global space permissions grant access unless explicitly restricted at space level. The engineering group has a global Can Use permission which allows them to view any space that does not explicitly exclude them. To restrict the finance space: go to Space Settings > Permissions, remove the engineering group from View access, and ensure only finance-team and confluence-admins have View. The global permission alone does not override space-level restrictions - you need to explicitly remove groups.

Removed engineering group from the finance space permissions. Tested with an engineering account - they can no longer see the space. Compliance issue resolved. This permission model is confusing and not well documented.
